title: Coredumps inside containers
body:
### Feature request description

When a program fatally fails inside a container && host runs with systemd (`core_pattern` is configured to systemd-coredump), the actual coredump ends up on the host which is inconvenient for debugging. (And [core_pattern cannot be virtualized](https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/6528).)


### Suggest potential solution

systemd (since v255) supports new directive `CoredumpReceive=yes`. That shall be configured on the host when the container is supposed to run own instance of systemd inside -- the coredump data (originating from container processes) are the forwarded to the internal instance and captured there.

It would be good if podman containers could be run with this directive enabled.


### Have you considered any alternatives?

- static core_pattern (dumps into a file relative to failing process CWD)
- setting `CoredumpReceive=yes` property manually for each container scope


### Additional context

- https://github.com/systemd/systemd/pull/29272
- this works primarily for containers set up as root but in principle (with some cascading) it could also work for rootless containers

comments.body: A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
